investment_decision.pivot: memory hierarchy and compute path optimization while preserving F32 correctness
investment_decision.first_experiment: F32 memory-hierarchy study: resident dense weights plus trace-driven expert-cache sizing

next_phase.prototype_gate: simulation reviewed before any runtime code change
success_gates: frozen F32 fixture IDs, router IDs, intermediate evidence and KV invariants remain unchanged
logical reads per token decrease materially in the modeled path
measurable cache hits occur at an explicitly bounded budget
resident bytes remain within each configured budget
no artifact duplication or canonical-root mutation
throughput is measured only after a reviewed memory-path prototype exists

frozen_invariants: canonical artifact and root manifest hashes/layout
ordered Rust F32 arithmetic and RMSNorm contract
higher-score then lower-expert-ID router tie ordering
F32 exact-safe router classifications at frozen guard layers
selected intermediate evidence and finite outputs
fixed-capacity KV cache allocation and no-overwrite guarantee
bounded memory and evidence schema compatibility
success_metrics: correctness status and first mismatch checkpoint
logical bytes per decode token and read amplification
peak resident and modeled explicit bytes
startup, prefill, and decode latency after prototype
